quotes: Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**.
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section

rules.intent: Maintain typographic hierarchy and readability by avoiding over-emphasis.
rules.rule_text: Bold is reserved strictly for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: heading(s), one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold more than ~15% of running body text in any section, and never bold entire paragraphs.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Ensure consistent brand prominence and professional compliance formatting.
rules.rule_text: Always bold callout text, statistical callout numbers, and the first instance per section of the brand name within a benefit claim. Never bold footnotes/references, prepositional filler, or the generic name.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Avoid visual noise and maintain accessibility compliance.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Limit styling to at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Avoid confusing users with non-functional underlines and maintain high legibility for patients.
rules.rule_text: Do not underline any text unless it is a real hyperlink. Do not use italic for emphasis in patient copy; reserve italics exclusively for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations.
